---
title: "MR"
author: "Yon Ho Jee"
date: "2026-01-04"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
library(data.table);library(dplyr);library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2);library(tidyverse);library(readxl)
```

# Exposure=Hyperthyroidism (Graves disease)
FinnGen-MVP-UKBB meta-analysis (https://public-mvp-ukbb.finngen.fi/pheno/E4_THYTOXGOITDIF)
```{r}
exposure_leadSNPs <- fread("~/Thyroid/replication/E4_THYTOXGOITDIF.tsv") #hg38
```

# Exposure=TSH
ThyroidOmics Consortium website (https://genepi.med.uni-greifswald.de/thyroidomics/)
```{r}
exposure_leadSNPs <- fread("~/Thyroid/MR/Sterenborg_2024_TSH_leadSNPs.txt") %>% 
  rename(chrom=Chr, pos=Position, ref=A2, alt=A1, rsids=`Genetic variant`, nearest_genes=`Nearest Gene`, most_severe=Function, maf=AF1, beta=Effect, se=SE, pval=P) #hg19
```

# Outcome=ThC
```{r}
meta_studies = "KCPS2_GBMI_FinnGen_MVP_ukb_out_GHI_LAT_PMBB"
dir1 <- "~/Thyroid/sumstats/"
meta_res <- fread(paste0(dir1,"meta_",meta_studies,"_ThC_hg38.txt.gz"), data.table = F) 
```

# MR
```{r}
# devtools::install_github("MRCIEU/MRInstruments")
# devtools::install_github("MRCIEU/TwoSampleMR")
library(MRInstruments);library(TwoSampleMR)

## ---------- Helpers ----------
# Compute SE from beta and two-sided p (when SE not available)
p_to_se <- function(beta, p) {
  z <- qnorm(p / 2, lower.tail = FALSE)
  abs(beta) / z
}

# Identify strand-ambiguous palindromic SNPs
is_palindromic <- function(a1, a2) {
  paste0(a1, a2) %chin% c("AT","TA","CG","GC")
}

## ---------- Exposure: build instrument dataset from lead SNPs ----------
LEAD <- as.data.table(exposure_leadSNPs)

# Standardize columns expected from your lead file (chrom/pos/ref/alt/rsids/beta/pval/maf)
stopifnot(all(c("chrom","pos","ref","alt","rsids","beta","pval") %in% names(LEAD)))

LEAD[, `:=`(
  chrom = as.integer(chrom),
  pos   = as.integer(pos),
  ref   = toupper(ref),
  alt   = toupper(alt),
  rsids = as.character(rsids)
)]

# Prefer SE if present; otherwise infer from beta+pval
if (!("se" %in% names(LEAD))) {
  LEAD[, se := p_to_se(beta, pval)]
}
```

```{r}
# Exposure dataset formatted for TwoSampleMR
exp_dat <- LEAD[, .(
  SNP = rsids,                                # use rsID for MR; fallback handled below if missing
  chr = chrom,
  pos = pos,
  effect_allele.exposure = alt,
  other_allele.exposure  = ref,
  eaf.exposure = if ("maf" %in% names(LEAD)) maf else NA_real_,
  beta.exposure = beta,
  se.exposure   = se,
  pval.exposure = pval,
  exposure = "TSH" #"Hyperthyroidism"
)]

# If any rsids missing/blank, create an ID so they can still be tracked
exp_dat[is.na(SNP) | SNP == "", SNP := paste0(chr, ":", pos, ":", other_allele.exposure, ":", effect_allele.exposure)]
```

```{r}
## ---------- Outcome: extract matching SNPs from meta_res ----------
ME <- as.data.table(meta_res)

# Standardize
stopifnot(all(c("CHR","POS","EFFECT_ALLELE","OTHER_ALLELE","BETA","SE","P") %in% names(ME)))
ME[, `:=`(
  CHR = as.integer(CHR),
  POS = as.integer(POS),
  EFFECT_ALLELE = toupper(EFFECT_ALLELE),
  OTHER_ALLELE  = toupper(OTHER_ALLELE)
)]

# Join by position first (robust to SNP naming differences)
setkey(exp_dat, chr, pos)
setkey(ME, CHR, POS)
m <- ME[exp_dat, on = .(CHR = chr, POS = pos), nomatch = 0]

# Keep allele-consistent matches (aligned or swapped); drop mismatches
m[, match_type := fifelse(
  EFFECT_ALLELE == effect_allele.exposure & OTHER_ALLELE == other_allele.exposure, "aligned",
  fifelse(EFFECT_ALLELE == other_allele.exposure & OTHER_ALLELE == effect_allele.exposure, "swapped",
          "mismatch")
)]
m <- m[match_type %chin% c("aligned","swapped")]
```

```{r}
# align outcome beta to exposure effect allele
m[, `:=`(
  beta.outcome = fifelse(match_type == "aligned", as.numeric(BETA), -as.numeric(BETA)),
  se.outcome   = as.numeric(SE),
  pval.outcome = as.numeric(P),
  eaf.outcome  = if ("Freq1" %in% names(m)) as.numeric(Freq1) else NA_real_,
  outcome      = "Thyroid cancer",
  N.outcome    = if ("N" %in% names(m)) as.numeric(N) else NA_real_
)]

# Build analysis table with BOTH exposure + outcome columns
dat_tmp <- m[, .(
  SNP,
  chr = as.integer(CHR),
  pos = as.integer(POS),

  effect_allele.exposure,
  other_allele.exposure,
  beta.exposure,
  se.exposure,
  pval.exposure,
  eaf.exposure,
  exposure,

  effect_allele.outcome = effect_allele.exposure,   # by construction after alignment
  other_allele.outcome  = other_allele.exposure,
  beta.outcome,
  se.outcome,
  pval.outcome,
  eaf.outcome,
  outcome,
  N.outcome
)]
```

```{r}
## ---------- Optional: conservative palindromic filter if EAF available ----------
pal <- is_palindromic(dat_tmp$effect_allele.exposure, dat_tmp$other_allele.exposure)
mid_af <- !is.na(dat_tmp$eaf.exposure) & dat_tmp$eaf.exposure > 0.42 & dat_tmp$eaf.exposure < 0.58
if (any(pal & mid_af)) {
  message("Dropping palindromic SNPs with intermediate exposure EAF: ", sum(pal & mid_af))
  dat_tmp <- dat_tmp[!(pal & mid_af)]
}
```

```{r}
## ---------- Harmonise & MR ----------
# Convert to TwoSampleMR format objects
dat_tmp <- as.data.frame(dat_tmp)

exp_fmt <- TwoSampleMR::format_data(
  dat_tmp,
  type = "exposure",
  snp_col = "SNP",
  beta_col = "beta.exposure",
  se_col = "se.exposure",
  effect_allele_col = "effect_allele.exposure",
  other_allele_col  = "other_allele.exposure",
  eaf_col  = "eaf.exposure",
  pval_col = "pval.exposure",
  chr_col  = "chr",
  pos_col  = "pos"
)

out_fmt <- TwoSampleMR::format_data(
  dat_tmp,
  type = "outcome",
  snp_col = "SNP",
  beta_col = "beta.outcome",
  se_col = "se.outcome",
  effect_allele_col = "effect_allele.outcome",
  other_allele_col  = "other_allele.outcome",
  eaf_col  = "eaf.outcome",
  pval_col = "pval.outcome",
  chr_col  = "chr",
  pos_col  = "pos"
)

```

```{r}
dat_h <- harmonise_data(exp_fmt, out_fmt, action = 2)  # action=2 drops incompatible/palindromic problematic SNPs

# Run MR methods
mr_res <- mr(dat_h, method_list = c(
  "mr_ivw",
  "mr_egger_regression",
  "mr_weighted_median",
  "mr_weighted_mode"
))
```

```{r}
# Sensitivity
het <- mr_heterogeneity(dat_h)
pleio <- mr_pleiotropy_test(dat_h)
loo <- mr_leaveoneout(dat_h)

print(mr_res)
print(het)
print(pleio)
```

```{r}
## ---------- Plots ----------
loo_plot <- mr_leaveoneout_plot(loo)
scat_plot <- mr_scatter_plot(mr_res, dat_h)
forest_plot <- mr_forest_plot(mr_singlesnp(dat_h))
```

# MRPRESSO
```{r}
# if (!require("devtools")) { install.packages("devtools") } else {}
# devtools::install_github("rondolab/MR-PRESSO")
library(MRPRESSO)

res_presso <- mr_presso(BetaOutcome = "beta.outcome", BetaExposure = "beta.exposure", SdOutcome = "se.outcome", SdExposure = "se.exposure", OUTLIERtest = TRUE, DISTORTIONtest = TRUE, data = dat_h, NbDistribution = 10000,  SignifThreshold = 0.05, seed = 20260309)
```

## Re-estimate IVW after removing MR-PRESSO outliers
```{r}
# 1) Pull MR-PRESSO outlier indices (these are row indices in the data passed to mr_presso)
outlier_idx <- res_presso$`MR-PRESSO results`$`Distortion Test`$`Outliers Indices`

# Safety checks
stopifnot(is.numeric(outlier_idx))
stopifnot(all(outlier_idx >= 1), all(outlier_idx <= nrow(dat_h)))

# 2) Create filtered dataset (remove outlier rows)
dat_h_no_outliers <- dat_h[-outlier_idx, , drop = FALSE]

cat("Original SNPs:", nrow(dat_h), "\n")
cat("Outliers removed:", length(outlier_idx), "\n")
cat("Remaining SNPs:", nrow(dat_h_no_outliers), "\n")

# 3) Re-run MR (IVW only, plus optional other methods for comparison)
mr_ivw_only <- mr(dat_h_no_outliers, method_list = "mr_ivw")
print(mr_ivw_only)

# Optional: compare to original IVW on full set
mr_ivw_full <- mr(dat_h, method_list = "mr_ivw")

# Put side-by-side
mr_ivw_compare <- rbind(
  transform(mr_ivw_full, dataset = "Full (all SNPs)"),
  transform(mr_ivw_only, dataset = "MR-PRESSO outliers removed")
)

# Keep just key columns (adjust if your TwoSampleMR version uses slightly different names)
keep_cols <- intersect(
  c("dataset", "id.exposure", "id.outcome", "exposure", "outcome", "method", "nsnp", "b", "se", "pval"),
  names(mr_ivw_compare)
)

mr_ivw_compare <- mr_ivw_compare[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE]
print(mr_ivw_compare)

# 4) (Recommended) Recompute heterogeneity and pleiotropy diagnostics after outlier removal
het_no_outliers <- mr_heterogeneity(dat_h_no_outliers)
print(het_no_outliers)

pleio_no_outliers <- mr_pleiotropy_test(dat_h_no_outliers)
print(pleio_no_outliers)

# 5) (Optional) Save the filtered harmonized dataset for reproducibility
# write.csv(dat_h_no_outliers, "harmonized_no_presso_outliers.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
mr_res_no_outliers <- mr(dat_h_no_outliers, method_list = c(
  "mr_ivw",
  "mr_egger_regression",
  "mr_weighted_median",
  "mr_weighted_mode"
))
mr_res_no_outliers

het <- mr_heterogeneity(dat_h_no_outliers)
pleio <- mr_pleiotropy_test(dat_h_no_outliers)
loo <- mr_leaveoneout(dat_h_no_outliers)

mr_leaveoneout_plot(loo)
mr_scatter_plot(mr_res_no_outliers, dat_h_no_outliers)
mr_forest_plot(mr_singlesnp(dat_h_no_outliers))
```
